g.text: Self-Pipe Trick Architecture: Async-Signal-Safe Signal Handling

ol.li: SIGCHLD Arrival: Kernel interrupts any current execution to run handler.
Minimal Handler: Handler does only the bare minimum: write a byte to a pipe. This is Async-Signal-Safe.
Main Loop Wakeup: The select() call (part of the normal event loop) sees the read-end of the pipe is now ready.
Context Switch: Execution moves from the dangerous signal context back to the safe main-loop context.
Heavy Lifting: Main loop drains the pipe and reaps all children using waitpid(). Now it's safe to use malloc, printf, or complex data structures.

div.p: ⚠️ DESIGN INVARIANT A signal can arrive while malloc() is holding a lock. If the handler calls malloc() again, it will DEADLOCK.
The Self-Pipe trick converts an asynchronous signal into a synchronous file-descriptor event.
SIGCHLD Child process state change (exit, stop, continue)
CRITICAL CONSTRAINT Handler can ONLY call async-signal-safe functions:

text.tspan: // Select blocks until data arrives in pipe
// Select blocks until data arrives in pipe

div.p: SAFE CONTEXT Now in main thread, we can call anything:
